support.title: Pod2Swim — Support
support.metaDesc: Support and help for Pod2Swim, the iOS app that loads podcasts onto your swim headset.
support.headerSubtitle: Podcasts on your swim headset — help & answers
support.contactText: Questions, bugs, or feature ideas? Email us and we'll get back to you.

gettingStarted.a: It loads podcast episodes onto a Shokz OpenSwim / OpenSwim Pro — or any MP3-player headset that mounts as USB storage — so you can listen while you swim, where Bluetooth doesn't reach. Subscribe, queue episodes, and the app downloads them and copies them straight onto the headset in playback order.

gettingStarted.q: How do I get episodes onto my headset?
gettingStarted.a: Subscribe to a podcast, add the episodes you want to the Queue, and wait for them to finish downloading (each row shows its status). Plug the headset into your iPhone with a USB‑C or Lightning adapter, open the Queue tab, and tap Export. The first time, pick the headset's folder in the Files picker — the app remembers it after that.
An iPhone running iOS 17 or later, an internet connection for downloads, and a USB‑C or Lightning adapter so the headset shows up as readable storage in the Files picker.

using.q: Replace All vs. Add to End — what's the difference?
using.a: Replace All deletes the audio files already on the headset and writes a fresh playlist from your queue. Add to End keeps what's there and appends your queue after it. You're asked each time, with a "don't ask again" option that remembers your choice.

using.q: Why don't my episodes play in the right order?
using.a: OpenSwim Pro plays files in the order they were transmitted, which maps to their file timestamps. Pod2Swim stamps each exported file in ascending order by queue position, so position 1 plays first. If order still looks wrong, re-export with Replace All so the headset starts from a clean set.

Yes. Each podcast has a speed setting from 0.75× to 2.5×. When an episode downloads, it's processed into a sped‑up file with the pitch preserved, and that's what gets exported. Set the default for new subscriptions in Settings, or override it per podcast in the Library.

using.q: The headset isn't detected when I tap Export or Scan.
using.a: Make sure the headset is plugged in and powered on, and that it appears under "Locations" in the Files app. If the saved folder can't be reached, the app falls back to the Files picker — re‑select the headset's folder there. Some adapters need to be seated firmly; try reconnecting the cable.

using.q: How do I free up storage?
using.a: Removing an episode from the queue deletes its downloaded files. Settings shows total downloaded storage with a Delete All Downloads button, and an auto‑delete window that removes exported files a set number of days after export (7 days by default).

support.privacyBlurb: Pod2Swim collects no personal data — no account, no analytics, no ads. Everything stays on your device. Read the full privacy policy.

privacy.title: Pod2Swim — Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDesc: Privacy policy for Pod2Swim. The app collects no personal data.

sections.body: Pod2Swim does not collect, store, or transmit any personal data. The app has no account system, no analytics, and no advertising.

Everything the app creates — your podcast subscriptions, your export queue, and the audio it downloads — is stored locally on your iPhone and never leaves it except as you direct (for example, when you copy episodes onto your own headset).

The app makes network requests only to:

  • Search the public Apple Podcasts directory when you look for a podcast to subscribe to.
  • Fetch the RSS feeds of podcasts you subscribe to.
  • Download the episodes you add to your queue.

These requests go directly to those third‑party servers (Apple and the podcast hosts). No data passes through any server operated by the developer, because there is no such server.

Podcast feeds and episodes are provided by third parties and are subject to their own privacy practices. Pod2Swim only retrieves the content you choose; it does not share any information about you with them beyond what is technically required to make the request.
Pod2Swim does not knowingly collect any information from anyone, including children.
If this policy changes, the updated version will be posted on this page with a new "last updated" date.
